channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: At meeting with Trump, Zelenskyy will seek security assurances against future Russian aggression
item.description: Ukraine's leader will meet with President Donald Trump in Washington on Friday at a pivotal moment for his country, one that hinges on whether he can persuade Trump to provide some form of U.S. backing for Ukraine's security against any future Russian aggression.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE and AAMER MADHANI Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Attempted murder fugitive busted as 40-year scheme posing as dead college mate unravels
item.description: A fugitive on the run for the attempted murder his estranged wife more than 40 years ago has been captured after his con of assuming a dead man’s identity for decades finally unraveled, the Justice Department announced Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Leonard Peltier remains defiant in AP interview, maintaining innocence and vowing continued activism
item.description: BELCOURT, N.D. (AP) — More than 50 years after a shootout on the Pine Ridge Indian Reservation landed him in federal prison, Leonard Peltier remains defiant.
item.dc:creator: GRAHAM LEE BREWER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump's early backing of Ramaswamy for Ohio governor seen by some as meant to avoid a nasty primary
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (AP) — The sun hadn't set on Republican Vivek Ramaswamy's gubernatorial campaign launch in Ohio earlier this week before President Donald Trump posted his endorsement of the Cincinnati-born biotech entrepreneur and former Department of Government Efficiency co-chair.
item.dc:creator: JULIE CARR SMYTH, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Volunteers use bullhorns and sirens to warn immigrants when ICE is in their area
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Just before dawn, 10 people met at a parking lot shared by a laundromat and coffee shop in South Central Los Angeles on what has become a daily mission: Look for immigration officers and warn people of their presence to try to prevent arrests.
item.dc:creator: DORANY PINEDA and ELLIOT SPAGAT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mourners bury one of the last hostages released from Gaza as talks start for ceasefire future
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Mourners in Israel on Friday were burying the remains of one of the last hostages released in the first phase of the ceasefire between Hamas militants and Israel, as negotiators discussed a second phase of the deal that could end the war in Gaza and see the remaining live hostages r
item.dc:creator: JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Georgia deputy who fatally shot exonerated man during traffic stop won't be charged
item.description: District Attorney Keith Higgins announced, following the GBI's findings, they will not seek criminal charges against a Georgia sheriff’s deputy involved in a 2023 fatal shooting.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Cardinal McElroy calls for compassion in immigration policy as he prepares to lead in Washington
item.description: Cardinal Robert McElroy, who is getting ready to take over as archbishop of Washington, D.C. in March, called for compassion and dignity for migrants Thursday.
item.dc:creator: DEEPA BHARATH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Iowa gives final approval to a bill removing gender identity protections despite protests
item.description: DES MOINES, Iowa (AP) — Iowa lawmakers became the first in the nation to approve legislation removing gender identity protections from the state’s civil rights code Thursday, despite massive protests by opponents who say it could expose transgender people to discrimination in numerous areas of life.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH FINGERHUT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Louisiana counseling service to pay millions in Medicaid fraud case
item.description: An investigation revealed that Community Health allegedly created a scheme to defraud the Medicaid program by offering financial incentives to Medicaid recipients in exchange for their patient information.
item.dc:creator: Dominique Woods, NewsNation

item.title: King Charles invites Trump to UK for a state visit
item.description: King Charles III has invited President Trump for a state visit to the United Kingdom, British Prime Minister Keir Starmer told the president during their meeting Thursday in Washington.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Former sheriff ‘flabbergasted’ by alleged gun seizure incident
item.description: “It sounds like robbery,” Wilson’s attorney, Bobby Bailey, said. “To go to a person’s personal job and to take his personal items; to me, that’s robbery.
item.dc:creator: Chris Hayes, NewsNation

item.title: Map: Listeria strain in deadly outbreak found as death toll climbs
item.description: An Indiana-based dairy company has recalled potentially affected shakes that were distributed nationwide to long-term care facilities.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, NewsNation

item.title: Students escape school bus fire in Ohio
item.description: An emergency broke out Thursday morning on a school bus carrying children in Cleveland Heights, Ohio. An unexpected blaze — requiring a quick evacuation.
item.dc:creator: Celeste Houmard, KRON4

item.title: NOAA begins firing hundreds of staffers
item.description: The National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) began the process of firing hundreds of probationary workers Thursday, the latest mass job cuts within federal agencies, three sources with knowledge of internal operations told The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Zack Budryk, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Man on 'Most Wanted' list for over 4 decades caught in New Mexico
item.description: After more than four decades, Stephen Craig Campbell, one of the United States' most wanted men, is now behind bars.
item.dc:creator: Bela Olague, WGN 9

item.title: USAID workers will be given 15 minutes to clear their workspaces as the agency gets dismantled
item.description: Thousands of U.S. Agency for International Development workers who have been fired or placed on leave as part of the Trump administration's dismantling of the agency are being given a brief window Thursday and Friday to clear out their workspaces.
item.dc:creator: GARY FIELDS Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Pope Francis continues to improve but his prognosis remains guarded: Vatican
item.description: Pope Francis continued his slow recovery from double pneumonia on Thursday, beating back speculation of an imminent death, resignation or conclave and signalling that he was still very much in charge albeit in a weakened state.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Oscar-winner Gene Hackman, his wife and their dog found dead in their New Mexico home
item.description: The gruff-but-beloved Hackman was among the finest actors of his generation, appearing as both villains, heroes and antiheroes in dozens of dramas, comedies and action films from the 1960s until his retirement in the early 2000s.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Princeton preppy accused in soccer star brother's gruesome murder heads to court: What to know
item.description: Matthew Hertgen, the Princeton, New Jersey, man accused of killing his brother, Joseph Hertgen, and a family cat appeared in court for the first time Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Police killings set record in 2024, but with smallest increase in years: Report
item.description: In 2024, police officers in the US killed 1,365 people, a slight increase from the 1,329 civilians killed in 2023, according to the Mapping Police Violence report.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, Fox 5

item.title: Bell tolls as New York marks the anniversary of the 1993 World Trade Center bombing
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York City on Wednesday marked the anniversary of the 1993 bombing at the old World Trade Center, when Islamic extremists first attempted to bring down the twin towers with 1,200 pounds of explosives in a parked rental van.
item.dc:creator: JOHN MINCHILLO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Super Bowl sports reporter found dead in hotel had sedative in his system: authorities
item.description: The woman accused of taking the reporter's phone and bank cards has previously been accused of drugging men to steal from them, according to investigators.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Hamilton, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court hears arguments on straight woman's 'reverse discrimination' case
item.description: Marlean Ames filed suit against a state agency claiming she was not given a promotion because she is heterosexual.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Democratic governors pitch state jobs to federal workers cut by Trump
item.description: ALBANY, N.Y. (AP) — Some of America's governors — mostly Democrats — have a message for the wave of fired federal workers: We want you.
item.dc:creator: ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Christianity in US stops decline after years of losses: Survey
item.description: Although down substantially from 2007, 44 percent of Americans said they pray at least once a day, a figure that has remained consistent in the last four years.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Supreme Court seems likely to rule for Ohio woman claiming job bias because she's straight
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court seemed likely Wednesday to side with an Ohio woman who claims she suffered sex discrimination from her employer because she is straight.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Woman accused of marrying her biological father
item.description: A Tell City woman is facing charges in Vanderburgh County for allegedly marrying her biological father.
item.dc:creator: Zach McKnight, WFLA, NBC

item.title: American Airlines flight discontinues landing to avoid departing plane in Virginia
item.description: The maneuver occurred less than two hours before another plane attempting to land at Chicago’s Midway Airport was forced to climb back into the sky to avoid another aircraft crossing the runway.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Child becomes first measles death reported in Texas outbreak
item.description: An unvaccinated school-aged child who was hospitalized with measles has passed away, marking the first death in the outbreak, according to Lubbock and state health officials.
item.dc:creator: Caitlyn Rooney, Fox 5

item.title: As DeSantis talks up his wife as next Florida governor, here's what to know about Casey
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (AP) — The aspirations of Florida’s first lady Casey DeSantis have long been a topic of discussion in the state capital of Tallahassee. This week, her husband Gov. Ron DeSantis added to the speculation by talking her up as his potential successor.
item.dc:creator: KATE PAYNE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: WATCH: Man caught hiding $10K of cocaine in toupée
item.description: A man was arrested in Columbia after trying to board a flight while wearing a wig hiding cocaine worth more than $10,000.
item.dc:creator: Celeste Houmard, WFLA, NBC

item.title: SWAT team raided the wrong Denver apartment and traumatized two young girls, lawsuit says
item.description: DENVER (AP) — Kirsty Shelton was at home preparing lunch and watching television with her daughters and their grandmother when they were startled by loud banging on their apartment door by a SWAT team looking for a man who lived across the hall.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN SLEVIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Inmate death reported as illegal prison guard strike stretches into 2nd week
item.description: COXSACKIE, N.Y. (AP) — A wildcat strike by guards at New York's prisons, now in its second week, is fueling fears over deteriorating conditions behind bars.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL HILL, CEDAR ATTANASIO and JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4
